component.name: Ants Terminal
component.summary: Modern, themeable terminal emulator with ligatures, inline images, and Lua plugins

description.p: Ants Terminal is a fast, feature-rich terminal emulator written from scratch in C++20 with Qt6. It targets the capabilities modern terminal users expect — true-color themes, ligatures, hyperlinks, inline images, and a scriptable plugin system — without dragging in a web runtime.
Key features:

ul.li: Eleven built-in themes with full ANSI palette overrides, plus automatic dark/light switching that follows the system color scheme.
Ligature-aware QPainter text shaping (HarfBuzz) with partial-rect updates for sub-millisecond redraws on noisy output.
Inline images via Sixel, Kitty graphics protocol, and iTerm2 OSC 1337. Image paste auto-saves to disk — optimized for LLM / coding-agent workflows.
Shell integration via OSC 133 (prompt jump, exit-status marks). OSC 8 hyperlinks, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 9/777 desktop notifications, OSC 9;4 progress reporting.
Kitty keyboard protocol with progressive enhancement, synchronized output, SGR mouse reporting, focus reporting.
Embedded Lua 5.4 plugin system (sandboxed) with documented ants.* API. Session persistence saves scrollback across restarts.
Built-in Project Audit panel: clazy + cppcheck + semgrep + regex rules with SARIF v2.1.0 export, AI-assisted triage, and confidence scoring.
Configurable keybindings, command snippets, auto-profile rules, SSH bookmarks, and an OpenAI-compatible AI assistant dialog.

screenshot.caption: Main terminal — custom title bar, tab gradient, status bar
Review Changes dialog — per-file diff and apply flow
Project Audit panel — clazy, cppcheck, and grep findings with SARIF export

description.p: Bundle G Tier 2 heavy-items closeout. ANTS-1135 (post-fork setenv signal-safety: pre-fork envp build + execle in child, replacing the prior execlp+setenv-loop pattern that was not on POSIX async-signal-safe list); ANTS-1133 partial (CSI Pn I / Z / ` — CHT / CBT / HPA verbs added on top of REP from 0.7.69); ANTS-1139 partial (markdown bold rendered, tables render as <table> not <pre>); ANTS-1140 partial (reverseTopLevelSections cache).

Bundle G Tier 2 final closeouts. ANTS-1134 (span cache invalidation across scrollback push), ANTS-1138 (auto-profile-rules pattern cache invalidation via generation counter), ANTS-1142 (KDE-presence guard lifted into shared kwinPresent() helper used by KWinPositionTracker + moveViaKWin + centerWindow), ANTS-1144 (Claude transcript render cap at last 2000 entries). ANTS-1133 partial — CSI Pn b (REP) handler shipped; other CSI-spec gaps remain deferred.

Bundle G Tier 2 closeouts — knocking off the deferred sub-fixes annotated in 0.7.67. ANTS-1136 (5/5 — RuleQualityTracker durability), ANTS-1141 (5/5 — parent-dir fsync after rename), ANTS-1142 (3/4 — MainWindow listens to sessionFailed with status-bar fallback), ANTS-1144 (2/3 — BgTasks dialog ANSI/SGR/OSC strip in tail output).

Bundle G Tier 2 progress sweep. 8 ANTS IDs touched: ANTS-1134 (modifier-only keypress guard), ANTS-1136 (audit pipeline doc + correctness — 4/5 sub-fixes), ANTS-1137 (MainWindow chrome perf), ANTS-1138 (applyTheme early-return), ANTS-1141 (Config + persistence — 4/5 sub-fixes), ANTS-1142 (Wayland portal + debug log — 2/4), ANTS-1143 (PLUGINS.md spec drift), ANTS-1144 (AI dialog partial-stream fail-closed — 1/3). Remaining Tier 2 sub-fixes deferred to 0.7.68 with explicit annotations in ROADMAP.

Bundle G Tier 1 remainder. Two HIGH fixes from the 2026-05-01 indie-review sweep — ANTS-1131 (PTY child-process lifecycle: WNOHANG=0 orphan + m_planModeByPid PID-reuse poisoning) and ANTS-1132 (IPC socket trust-model gaps: SO_PEERCRED UID match on remote-control accept, UserAccessOption + lstat-checked S_ISSOCK precheck on Claude hook + MCP server starts, per-connection idle timeout). Tier 2 + Tier 3 follow in subsequent bumps.

Bundle G Tier 1 fix-pass. Two CRITICAL fixes from the 2026-05-01 multi-agent indie-review sweep — ANTS-1118 (smooth-scroll snapshot race during streaming, root cause identified by the terminal-widget-paint reviewer) and ANTS-1130 (VT alt-screen + scroll-region invariants — DECSET 47/1047/1049 conflation, resize() destroying DECSTBM, alt scroll-region OOB on shrink-resize). Two more Tier 1 items (ANTS-1131 PTY child lifecycle + ANTS-1132 IPC socket trust gaps) defer to 0.7.66 to keep this release's blast radius small.
Fixed:

ul.li: Smooth-scroll snapshot race — wheelEvent now triggers snapshot capture + scrollback-insertion pause on user scroll INTENT rather than committed offset transition. Closes a 16–32 ms race window where the first smoothScrollStep tick produced no offset change, letting onVtBatch mutate visible viewport rows before the snapshot was captured. (ANTS-1118)

VT alt-screen + scroll-region invariants — split DECSET 47/1047/1049 (only 1049 carries DECSC semantics per xterm ctlseqs); resize() preserves DECSTBM by clamping existing scroll region to the new row range instead of resetting to fullscreen; alt scroll-region clamped symmetrically (previously unclamped — could leave m_altScrollBottom > m_rows-1 and produce OOB reads on next scrollUp after exit-from-alt). (ANTS-1130)

description.p: Companion partial closeouts pre-Bundle-G. ANTS-1106 ships the Roadmap viewer's Kind-faceted secondary filter — the last half of the "mandatory Kind: field + viewer faceted categorisation" item, with the field-required half having landed in 0.7.59 alongside the standards doc update. Three status hygiene flips for ANTS-1116 v1 (ants-helper drift-check) and ANTS-1117 v1 (roadmap-query + tab-list IPC verbs) — both shipped in 0.7.59 but the status emoji never flipped. ANTS-1118 reframed from HIGH to MEDIUM after the user confirmed the bug downgraded post-0.7.63 (still a temporary visual artifact, but the scrollback buffer itself is intact).
Added:

ul.li: Roadmap viewer Kind-faceted filter — a new Kind row in RoadmapDialog underneath the existing status-checkbox row, with one toggle per Kind value (12 total — the 10 spec-defined plus the de-facto research / ux values). OR-include semantics across the active Kinds; bullets without a Kind: line are excluded under non-empty filters. (ANTS-1106)

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: ROADMAP status flips for ANTS-1116 v1 + ANTS-1117 v1 — both shipped in 0.7.59; the status emoji never flipped. v2 of each (MCP server wrapper + audit-run IPC verb) explicitly stays deferred per their specs, gated on ANTS-1120 measurement validation.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: ANTS-1118 severity downgraded HIGH → MEDIUM after user confirmation — scrollback buffer is intact, visible artifact is a paint-cycle race during streaming. Folded into Bundle G (full /audit + /indie-review) as a candidate finding for the multi-agent fresh-eyes pass.

description.p: Outstanding-queue cleanup pre-Bundle-G (full /audit + /indie-review). ANTS-1014 clipboard-write redaction lands as the one real code fix — centralises 15 raw setText(...) sites behind a single hygiene funnel, with OSC 52 (the headline exfil vector flagged by the 7th-audit memory) and Lua plugin ants.clipboard.write getting a 1 MiB cap + NUL strip while user-initiated copy actions pass through unchanged. Two regressions get explicit "awaiting repro" status flips so the upcoming review pass doesn't re-flag them as untreated bugs, and six big items get explicit deferral annotations with re-evaluation triggers so orphan planned bullets read as deferred-by-design rather than forgotten.
Added:

ul.li: Clipboard-write redaction funnel — new src/clipboardguard.{h,cpp} module with a pure sanitize(text, source) helper splitting on a Source enum (Trusted / UntrustedPty / UntrustedPlugin). Untrusted sources cap at 1 MiB; all sources strip QChar(0). All 15 raw QApplication::clipboard()->setText(...) sites in terminalwidget.cpp + mainwindow.cpp converted with appropriate source classifications. (ANTS-1014)

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: ROADMAP outstanding-queue cleanup — ANTS-1052 + ANTS-1118 flipped to "Awaiting user repro" status; ANTS-1043 / 1044 / 1003-1006 / 1107 / 1108 / 1115 annotated with explicit deferral rationale + re-evaluation triggers.

description.p: Bundle B of the pre-0.7.61 outstanding-item triage — two small UX wins that close ROADMAP carryover. The update-available indicator moves out of the status bar onto the menu bar so the one-shot "you have a new version" call-to-action reads as visually loud chrome instead of competing with the steady-state widgets, and the git-branch chip on the status bar gains a colour cue (green for main/master/trunk, amber for everything else) that pairs it visually with the existing "Public/Private" repo-visibility pill next to it.
Changed:

ul.li: Update-available indicator promoted from a status-bar QLabel to a top-level menu-bar QAction, inserted to the right of &Help. URL is stashed via QAction::setData; the triggered lambda replays it through the existing handleUpdateClicked slot — same in-place AppImageUpdate flow, same Update / Skip / Postpone dialog. Status bar reclaims its rightmost slot for steady-state telemetry. (ANTS-1124)

Git-branch chip on the status bar gains a branch-derived colour cue. New pure helper branchchip::isPrimaryBranch returns true for main / master / trunk, false for every other ref. Both styling sites (applyTheme for theme switches, updateStatusBar for per-poll branch changes) consult the helper and pick theme.ansi[2] (green, same role as the "Public" visibility pill) or theme.ansi[3] (amber, same role as "Private"). Visually pairs the chip with the pill; functionally tells the user at a glance whether they are on the project's primary branch. (ANTS-1109)

ROADMAP.md status flips for ANTS-1100 (RoadmapDialog faceted-tabs redesign) and ANTS-1119 (AuditEngine extraction) — both shipped in 0.7.59, status emoji never flipped. ANTS-1049 body trimmed to a "subsumed by ANTS-1044" note (same scope as the structural-tier auditdialog.cpp decomposition).

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Test tests/features/github_status_bar/ updated to reflect the ANTS-1124 migration: INV-1, INV-2, INV-11 now assert the new QAction shape plus a negative regression check that no m_updateAvailableLabel identifier survives anywhere in mainwindow.{h,cpp}.

description.p: Dialog-and-roadmap polish bundle — fixes three user-visible chrome bugs (theme propagation to dialogs, focus-return on dialog close, pseudo-modal click-blocking on the parent), splits the now-260 KiB ROADMAP.md by minor version so tooling can keep up, retires two long-deprecated standards docs, and folds in a batch of indie-review Tier-3 closures.
Added:

ul.li: Per-version ROADMAP archive — closed minors rotate to docs/roadmap/<MAJOR>.<MINOR>.md via packaging/rotate-roadmap.sh. The viewer pulls archives in only on demand (History preset OR non-empty search). Numeric (major, minor) descending sort defeats the lexical-sort trap at minor 10. Per-file 8 MiB cap + total 64 MiB cap. Initial archives at 0.5.md and 0.6.md. (ANTS-1125)

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Theme stylesheet now reaches top-level dialogs. Switched MainWindow::applyTheme from setStyleSheet(ss) on the MainWindow instance to qApp->setStyleSheet(ss) — Qt's stylesheet engine only propagates through a widget's render subtree, not through QObject parent-child relationships, so top-level QDialogs never inherited the theme. All 9 dialog classes plus 7 ad-hoc new QDialog(this) instances now match. (ANTS-1128)

Auto-return focus to terminal when any dialog closes. The qApp eventFilter gained a Close-event branch backed by a pure-logic helper dialogfocus::shouldRefocusOnDialogClose; refocus is deferred via QTimer::singleShot(0) so the dialog teardown completes first. Stacked-dialog suppression and early-startup null-guard included. (ANTS-1050)

Pseudo-modal blocking on the parent. 0.7.50 made all dialogs non-modal to dodge QTBUG-79126 on Wayland; click-blocking restored manually via dialogfocus::shouldSuppressEventForDialog. Mouse / key / wheel events landing outside any visible dialog's tree are swallowed. (ANTS-1051)
Menubar dropdown flicker confirmed resolved by the 0.7.5 NoAnimStyle plus HoverMove suppression — the KWin-side experimentation hypothesis didn't materialise. (ANTS-1058)

XcbPositionTracker renamed to KWinPositionTracker (the class talks to KWin's scripting D-Bus interface, not XCB). KDE-presence guard bails before any temp-file write on non-KDE compositors. QScopeGuard ensures the temp script is removed on every synchronous failure path. (ANTS-1045)

Post-fork heap allocations in flatpak-spawn argv build retired. Detection plus snprintf into stack buffers now happens pre-fork; the child only assembles a stack-allocated argv table and calls execvp — strictly POSIX async-signal-safe. (ANTS-1046)
RoadmapDialog::rebuild's readAll now caps at 8 MiB; refreshReviewButton deduplicates overlapping git status probes via m_reviewProbeInFlight. (ANTS-1012, ANTS-1013)

description.p: Removed:

ul.li: Deprecated top-level STANDARDS.md and RULES.md retired (user-authorised). Both predated the docs/standards/ bundle and duplicated content. Live references updated across CONTRIBUTING.md, PLUGINS.md, README.md, and the audit dialog's "Review with Claude" header. (ANTS-1105)

description.p: ANTS-1123 indie-review Tier 2 + Tier 3 fold-in — the remaining hardening + polish bullets from the 0.7.59 4-agent independent review of the Claude Code companion features. Cache contract on the roadmap-query IPC verb honours the spec "≤ 100 ms" budget literally; ants-helper rejects malformed JSON input shapes; AuditEngine::parseFindings no longer emits bogus SARIF locators for version-string tokens; RoadmapDialog gracefully handles a corrupt persisted geometry blob and preserves user state on Custom-tab clicks. CLAUDE.md streamlined.
Fixed:

ul.li: ants-helper now rejects non-object JSON request bodies ([], 42, "foo") with a usage-error envelope instead of silently treating them as {}; stdin-open failure surfaces a usage error rather than degrading to empty input. (F6, F7)

parseFindings regex tightened to require an alphabetic-led extension — bare version-string locators like cargo/1.75 no longer create bogus SARIF physicalLocation entries. Short-circuited the per-line regex chain (was matching three regexes per line; now one). (M1, L1)
roadmap-query cache now honours the spec INV-10 "≤ 100 ms" wall-clock bound on top of the existing mtime check, so an in-place edit within the same mtime tick is still picked up. (F1)

Roadmap dialog: corrupt persisted geometry blob is now cleared instead of masquerading as valid forever; clicking the "Custom" tab preserves user checkbox tuning and sort order; tab-order array gained a static_assert against the Preset enum size. (LOW-1, LOW-2, LOW-3)
ANTS-1117 spec § Error-response shape rewritten to the flat-envelope convention ({ok:true, bullets:[...]}) the codebase actually ships — earlier nested-on-data wording was the outlier. (F9)

description.p: Claude Code companion features — faceted preset tabs and search on the Roadmap viewer (ANTS-1100), two read-only IPC verbs Claude can drive (tab-list, roadmap-query; ANTS-1117 v1), an optional standalone CLI helper (ants-helper drift-check; ANTS-1116 v1), an audit-engine extraction that unblocks future non-GUI audit consumers (ANTS-1119 v1), and a measurement gate that grounds further companion work in real token counts (ANTS-1120). Bundled with documentation cold-eyes review (ANTS-1121), audit fold-in fixes (ANTS-1122), and Tier 1 indie-review fixes (ANTS-1123).
Added:

ul.li: Roadmap dialog faceted preset tabs — Full / History / Current / Next / Far Future / Custom — each mapping to a (filter, sort) preset evaluated in pure C++. New search box accepts substring or id:NNNN shorthand to jump to a specific bullet. Default size 1200×800 with persisted geometry round-trip via Config::roadmapDialogGeometry. (ANTS-1100)

Two new remote-control IPC verbs (read-only): roadmap-query returns parsed ROADMAP bullets (id, status, headline, kind, lanes); tab-list returns rich per-tab snapshots (index, title, cwd, shell_pid, claude_running, color). Both inherit the existing UID-scoped trust check. (ANTS-1117 v1)

Optional ants-helper CLI binary (built when CMake is invoked with -DANTS_ENABLE_HELPER_CLI=ON). v1 ships ants-helper drift-check, which wraps packaging/check-version-drift.sh and emits a unified JSON envelope Claude can parse without re-interpreting the script's stdout. Builds against Qt6::Core only. (ANTS-1116 v1)

AuditEngine GUI-free module — the data-shape types and three pure parsing functions extracted from auditdialog.cpp into src/auditengine.{h,cpp} depending on Qt6::Core only. Pure refactor; audit pipeline output is byte-identical pre/post. Unblocks future non-GUI audit consumers. (ANTS-1119 v1)

Companion-instrumentation harness (ANTS-1120) — scripts/measure-companion-tokens.sh skeleton plus journal placeholder for measuring per-call token savings of the helper-stub vs baseline configurations before any further companion bullets ship.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: Kind: field is now required on every actionable ROADMAP.md bullet (previously optional, inferred from section context). All 100 unannotated bullets backfilled. Spec updated at docs/standards/roadmap-format.md § 3.5 and synced to the user-level /start-app template. (ANTS-1106)

Documentation tree refresh (ANTS-1121, full-app cold-eyes review): retired GPU-renderer references from STANDARDS.md / RULES.md / PLUGINS.md (the glrenderer.cpp path was removed in 0.7.44); refreshed version stamps across PLUGINS.md, SECURITY.md, packaging/README.md, README.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md; relocated archival snapshots (DISCOVERY.md, AUDIT.md, FIXPLAN.md, project_audit_updates.md) from project root to docs/journal/ with historical-snapshot headers; retired the stale docs/AUTOMATED_AUDIT_REPORT.md copy.

Audit-engine fold-in fixes (ANTS-1122) — clazy range-loop-detach in antshelper.cpp and roadmapdialog.cpp::parseBullets; cppcheck constVariablePointer in auditengine.cpp::applyFilter; cppcheck returnByReference on MainWindow::roadmapPathForRemote(). Strengthened tests/features/roadmap_viewer_tabs/ with negative-case and parsed-numeric invariants.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Section-reference drift in newly-authored specs — docs/specs/ANTS-1117.md, docs/specs/ANTS-1120.md, and docs/decisions/0002-cold-eyes-companion-cleanup.md cited § 1.2 / § 3.1 / § 3.5 for facts that actually live in § 3.3 (status emojis) and § 3.5.1 (stable-ID immutability). Corrected during the cold-eyes cross-check pass.

description.p: Security:

ul.li: regex-DoS divergence closed. Pre-0.7.59 the engine extraction had left two independent definitions of isCatastrophicRegex / hardenUserRegex between auditengine.cpp and auditdialog.cpp with differing LIMIT_MATCH values (200000 vs 100000), no double-prefix guard on the engine version, and shape detectors that caught different sets of catastrophic-backtracking patterns. A pattern that triggered the bound on one path could slip through on the other. Unified to a single definition exported from the engine; both call sites delegate. (ANTS-1123 indie-review C1+C2+C3+H1)

description.p: Runtime toggle for the ANTS-1054 dialog tracer. The 0.7.57 env-var-only path required restarting Ants Terminal to diagnose the user-reported mystery flashing dialogs; the new menu action lets the user enable / disable the tracer mid-session without losing tab state.
Added:

ul.li: Tools → Debug Mode → "Trace dialog show events" — checkable menu action that toggles the same DialogShowTracer the ANTS_TRACE_DIALOGS=1 env var path installs. Output goes to stderr (always) and to the events debug-log category when enabled. Status-bar transient confirms the toggle. (ANTS-1054 follow-up)

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: DialogShowTracer extracted from src/main.cpp to its own src/dialogshowtracer.{h,cpp} translation unit so both the startup env-var path and the runtime menu toggle call one setActive(bool) entry point. Process-global instance is parented to qApp; idempotent install / uninstall.

description.p: Indie-review backlog cleanup — surgical Tier 3 fixes (shellQuote whitelist, claudeChildrenOf extraction), a diagnostic for the mystery flashing-dialog report, a cppcheck returnByReference perf sweep, and a ROADMAP audit that flipped 28 prior indie-review items from planned to done once verified shipped in 0.7.52–0.7.55.
Added:

ul.li: ANTS_TRACE_DIALOGS=1 env var installs a DialogShowTracer event filter on the QApplication. Logs every top-level QWidget/QDialog Show event with className, objectName, windowTitle, parent, and geometry to stderr. Diagnostic step (a) of ANTS-1054 — the user reported a small popup flashing periodically in the centre of the terminal; the trace attributes the next occurrence to either an Ants spawn site (logged) or a child process (silent trace). Zero cost when the env var is unset.

tests/features/shellutils_whitelist/ — feature test for the ANTS-1047 fix. Verifies four invariants: empty input maps to '', the whitelist [A-Za-z0-9_-./:@%+,] passes through, anything outside it forces single-quote wrapping (specifically the glob/redirection/bracket characters the old denylist missed), and embedded single quotes round-trip via the POSIX 'X'\''Y' form.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: ClaudeIntegration::findClaudeChildPid(pid_t) — extracted from the two near-identical /proc/<pid>/task/<pid>/children walks in pollClaudeProcess and ClaudeTabTracker::detectClaudeChild. Both call sites share one helper; detectClaudeChild also gains the /proc-scan fallback that previously only pollClaudeProcess had. (ANTS-1048)

Cppcheck returnByReference perf sweep across seven hot getters: Config::loadFailureBackupPath, ClaudeBgTaskTracker::transcriptPath, GhostLineEdit::ghostSuffix, TerminalGrid::windowTitle, TerminalWidget::shellTitle, TerminalWidget::imagePasteDir, PluginManager::pluginDir. Each was returning a QString by value — now returns const QString&.

description.p: Security:

ul.li: shellutils.h::shellQuote switched from a denylist regex to a whitelist regex. The denylist missed glob wildcards (*, ?), redirection (<, >), and bracket-expansion specials ([, ]); a path containing any of those reached the shell unquoted and underwent globbing. Whitelist closes the class — anything outside [A-Za-z0-9_-./:@%+,] gets single-quote wrapped. Affects every caller of shellQuote — mainwindow.cpp claude-launch builders and sshdialog.cpp ssh-argv construction. (ANTS-1047)

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: ROADMAP.md hygiene — flipped 28 [ANTS-1015..1042, 1047, 1048] bullets from planned to done after verifying each item shipped in the 0.7.52–0.7.55 cycle (or in this 0.7.57 bundle). Each done bullet now cites the shipped version and the source-comment line. ANTS-1054 flipped to in-progress with the diagnostic-shipped note. Tier 3 refactors ANTS-1043, 1044, 1045, 1046, 1049 remain planned (large-scope items deferred).

description.p: App-Build suite alignment — confirm-on-close for tabs running non-shell processes, four-doc shareable standards bundle synced to the user-level /start-app template, CI-blocking metainfo escape fix, and the strategic roadmap for incorporating the App-Build workflow natively into Ants Terminal so users can run as much of it as possible without spending Claude tokens.
Added:

ul.li: Confirm-on-close for tabs running non-shell processes (vim, top, claude, tail -f, ...). Wayland-correct non-modal dialog names the process; "Don't ask again" checkbox flips Config::confirmCloseWithProcesses to false. Default on. Probe walks /proc/<pid>/task/<pid>/children transitively (cap 256 visited PIDs) against an 11-shell allowlist (bash/zsh/fish/sh/ksh/dash/ash/tcsh/csh/mksh/yash). Locked by 11 invariants in tests/features/confirm_close_with_processes/.
Reopen-closed-tab via Ctrl+Shift+Z — surfaced as already-shipped during the confirm-on-close pass; m_closedTabs deque (cap 10) plus File → Undo Close Tab.

Four-document shareable standards bundle at docs/standards/ (coding · documentation · testing · commits) plus an index README, with the detailed ROADMAP/CHANGELOG format spec carried as a sub-spec at docs/standards/roadmap-format.md. Commits standard mandates <ID>: <description> subjects; testing standard mandates TDD by default.
ADR scaffolding at docs/decisions/ (Michael Nygard format) plus docs/specs/ and docs/journal/ placeholder folders.

Roadmap for incorporating the App-Build workflow natively into Ants Terminal (ANTS-1108) — Workflow panel that runs the mechanical 70 % of the per-phase 9-step loop in C++ (ID allocation, /audit, ctest, drift checks, /debt-sweep, /bump, /release, fold-in templating, atomic CHANGELOG/ROADMAP edits) with zero LLM round-trips.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: ROADMAP masthead now points to docs/standards/roadmap-format.md for the format spec; docs/standards/ is now byte-identical to the user-level /start-app template, with per-language idiom examples and push-policy details delegated to the global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md.

README.md project-structure tree adds docs/standards/, docs/decisions/, docs/specs/, docs/journal/; flags STANDARDS.md / RULES.md as deprecated (retire under ANTS-1105 with explicit user confirmation).
Roadmap-dialog redesign refined per user feedback — tabs now Full / History / Current / Next / Far Future, search field above the TOC, default size bumped to ~1200x800 with persisted geometry.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: CHANGELOG.md was missing a top-level [Unreleased] block that the Roadmap dialog reads for current-work signaling.
Unescaped & in the 0.7.55 metainfo <release> body broke appstreamcli validate and turned CI red on every commit since the release. Now &.

description.p: VT parser correctness + audit-dialog hardening from the 2026-04-27 indie-review. Six fixes — multi-row OSC 8 hyperlink span emission, ITU/ECMA-48 colon-RGB form parsing, audit comment-suppress hyphen handling, trend-snapshot dedup, bg-tasks liveness sweep split from full reparse, and a hot-path returnByReference fix.
Fixed:

ul.li: Multi-row OSC 8 hyperlink spans miscoded. Pre-fix a hyperlink that wrapped to multiple rows stored only a single span on the start row with the wrong endCol; now emits per-row spans on close. Pre-0.7.55 shape preserved for single-row spans + the resize-pushed-text-to-scrollback edge case.

ITU/ECMA-48 colon-RGB form 38:2::r:g:b dropped a channel. Parser now detects the 4-slot colon group (colorspace + R + G + B) and reads R/G/B from the right offsets. Legacy 38;2;r;g;b and 3-slot 38:2:r:g:b continue unchanged.
Audit comment-suppress regex mis-handled hyphenated rule IDs (// nosemgrep: bash-c-non-literal matched only bash). Terminator class shrunk to [)\]]|$; hyphenated IDs now suppress correctly.
Trend snapshot duplicated on every UI filter click. Gated by m_snapshotPersisted (reset in runAudit, set on first authoritative render). Re-renders triggered by severity-pill toggles skip the append.

bg-tasks: split liveness sweep from full transcript reparse. Added sweepLiveness() that walks m_tasks via stat() calls + mtime check; the 2 s status-timer drives this cheap sweep instead of the 16 MiB transcript reparse. File-watcher continues to drive full rescan() on transcript-changed.
ClaudeBgTaskTracker::tasks() returned by value on the hot path. cppcheck-flagged returnByReference. Now returns const &.

description.p: A11y + UX bundle from the 2026-04-27 indie-review. Six fixes — accessibility plumbing on status-bar surfaces and ToggleSwitch, plan-mode persistence across tab switches, an SSE iteration cap to prevent UI freeze on misbehaving AI endpoints, and IPv6 support in the SSH Quick Connect parser.
Changed:

ul.li: A11y — status-bar QLabels gain setAccessibleName. Branch chip, repo visibility, foreground-process, status-message slot, Claude session label, context bar, Review Changes button. Screen readers (Orca / NVDA / VoiceOver) now announce semantic text instead of raw tab content or Powerline glyph codepoints. Claude session label additionally updates accessibleDescription on every state transition.

A11y — ToggleSwitch a11y plumbing. Default accessibleName + QAccessibleStateChangeEvent on every check-state flip. Keyboard users now get immediate AT-SPI / UIAutomation confirmation when their toggle lands.

Plan-mode survives tab switch. setShellPid used to reset m_planMode = false on every pid change; now caches per-pid plan-mode state and restores it across tab flips so the indicator doesn't flicker off→on.
AI SSE parser caps per-tick iterations at 256 + re-arms via singleShot(0). Prevents UI freeze on misbehaving endpoints streaming millions of tiny lines in one buffer.
SSH Quick Connect parses IPv6 host literals. [2001:db8::1]:2222 used to parse as host=[2001 port=0; now matches RFC 3986 §3.2.2 / OpenSSH command-line convention.

description.p: Tier-1 remainders + VT/paste/plugin hardening from the 2026-04-27 indie-review. Six security-class fixes — three completing the Tier-1 ship-this-week list (the OSC-ESC parser RCE-adjacent leak, X10 mouse UTF-8 corruption, Lua plugin permission allow-list/intersect drift), three from Tier-2 paste/image hardening.
Fixed:

ul.li: HIGH — VT parser dispatched OSC's trailing ESC byte as a real ESC sequence. A crafted OSC ending in `ESC c` triggered RIS (full terminal reset); `ESC D` triggered IND; etc. RCE-adjacent because OSC payloads can arrive from a hostile remote shell or trigger-rule expansion. Fixed at the C0 pre-handler — string-state ESC no longer transitions to Escape; new *StringEsc peek-states consume the trailing byte. Locked by tests/features/vt_osc_esc_discard/ (5 invariants × 11 sub-cases).
HIGH — X10 mouse byte > 0xDF corrupted UTF-8 stream. Coordinates clamped to 223 in X10 mode. SGR mode unaffected.

HIGH — Lua plugin permission allow-list + intersect missing. Manifest entries now filtered against knownPermissions() (clipboard.write, settings); prompt-result intersected with requested set before reaching engine.
HIGH — Image-paste filename injection + path escape. m_imagePasteDir canonicalised + required under $HOME; UUID4 suffix on filename to prevent millisecond-collision clobbers.
HIGH — Paste preview split on LF only. Bare \r terminator spoofed multi-line payload as single line. Now normalises \r\n + \r to \n for display; actual paste still writes bytes verbatim.
HIGH — Bracketed-paste 8-bit C1 form \x9B[200~ not stripped. Now strips 7-bit (\x1B[), raw 8-bit (\x9B), and UTF-8-encoded 8-bit (\xC2\x9B) forms.

description.p: First Tier-1 sweep of the 2026-04-27 indie-review findings. Eight fixes covering CRITICAL data-loss / security regressions plus HIGH security-class hardening — SessionManager silent scrollback loss, the same Wayland modal-grab pattern from 0.7.50 lurking in the update-confirmation dialog, SARIF/HTML reports leaking secrets at 0644, and five smaller HIGH items. The user-reported GitHub repo-type chip regression is also resolved (verified visible).
Fixed:

ul.li: CRITICAL — SessionManager silent data loss. saveSession + saveTabOrder used QFile::rename, which on POSIX refuses to overwrite an existing destination. Every session save AFTER the first silently failed: the .dat file held the original snapshot, .dat.tmp accumulated each new write, user scrollback never updated past the first save. Switched both to std::rename (POSIX rename(2) — atomic replace), mirroring the 0.7.12 Config fix. Errno logged on failure; orphaned .tmp removed.

CRITICAL — Update-confirmation dialog same Wayland modal-grab regression as 0.7.50 About fix. handleUpdateClicked was a QMessageBox box(this); box.exec() — exactly the pattern that drops button clicks on KDE/KWin + frameless+translucent parent per QTBUG-79126 / QTBUG-90005. Converted to the proven non-modal QDialog + plain QPushButton + clicked→close shape, mirroring the 0.7.50 About-dialog fix.

CRITICAL — SARIF / HTML export not atomic + 0644 perms. The reports embed every finding including any leaked secret surfaced by the gitleaks / secrets_scan rules. Switched to QSaveFile + commit() (atomic write-rename-fsync) plus setOwnerOnlyPerms (0600).

HIGH — new-tab / launch IPC commands bypassed the send-text C0 filter. A same-UID attacker reaching the rc socket got ESC-sequence / OSC 52 / bracketed-paste primitives via a different command than the one that was hardened. Both now route through filterControlChars by default; both honour a raw:true opt-out.

HIGH — OSC 8 file:// and ftp:// schemes removed from the hyperlink allowlist. xdg-open file:///foo.desktop autoexecutes via the desktop's .desktop handler (RCE-adjacent); ftp:// drops credentials in plaintext. Allowlist now covers only http/https/mailto.

HIGH — SSH extraArgs quote-bypass on the dangerous-option allowlist. The previous regex split fractured quoted args, letting -o "ProxyCommand=…" silently bypass the case-insensitive ProxyCommand match. Switched to QProcess::splitCommand which mirrors POSIX shell quoting.
HIGH — extractCwdFromTranscript unbounded readLine. Caps readLine at 64 KiB so a corrupted transcript with a multi-GiB single-line head doesn't OOM the process.
HIGH — AI endpoint scheme allowlist (http/https only). Rejects file:// / gopher:// / bare-host schemes up-front rather than at request time when the body is already serialised.

HIGH — openFileAtPath argv-injection via attacker-controlled paths starting with -. The helper prepends ./ to single-arg combined-path branches and inserts -- before the path on separate-arg branches.

description.p: User reports resolved:

ul.li: GitHub Public/Private repo-type chip not showing — user confirmed via screenshot 2026-04-28 that the chip is now visible.

description.p: Hot-reload doesn't loop any more. Same-day follow-up to 0.7.50 — three reported symptoms (status bar permanently sticking at "Config reloaded from disk", Help → Check for Updates appearing to do nothing, Settings → Preferences not opening) all traced to a single root cause: MainWindow::onConfigFileChanged re-entering itself in an infinite inotify loop. The 0.7.31 attempt at fixing the same loop (m_configWatcher->blockSignals(true/false) bracketing) never worked because Qt reads inotify events from the event loop after the slot returns — outliving the blockSignals window. Fix is at the source: make the setters idempotent so the slot has nothing to re-trigger.
Fixed:

ul.li: Config reload re-entered itself in an inotify loop. Config::setTheme unconditionally called save() even when the value matched, so any applyTheme(m_config.theme()) from inside onConfigFileChanged rewrote the watched file → kernel inotify event → next event-loop tick re-emits fileChanged → slot re-enters → loop. setTheme now early-returns when the value matches, breaking the loop at its source. onConfigFileChanged additionally skips the no-op applyTheme call when the theme didn't change, and carries an m_inConfigReload re-entrancy flag (cleared via QTimer::singleShot(0, ...) so a save inside the slot is dropped but a subsequent genuine external edit is honored). The failed 0.7.31 blockSignals calls are removed. User-visible: status bar stops sticking, Help → Check for Updates produces a visible toast, Settings → Preferences opens. Locked down by tests/features/config_reload_loop_safety/ — INV-1 idempotent setter, INV-2 no-op skip, INV-3 re-entrancy guard, INV-4 failed blockSignals removed.

description.p: Round four on the same dialog-button bug — and the matching Roadmap-dialog Close button. Three prior fix attempts (0.7.22, 0.7.35, 0.7.49) each diagnosed a downstream symptom rather than the actual root cause; this release identifies it from upstream Qt bug reports and applies the fix that the bg-tasks dialog has been quietly using all along.
Fixed:

ul.li: Both Help → About OK buttons (still) didn't dismiss the dialog in 0.7.49, and the Roadmap dialog's Close button hadn't worked since 0.7.43 either. User report 2026-04-28: "OK buttons still do nothing on the 2 About dialogs… Close button does nothing on the Roadmap either." Real root cause: both upstream Qt bugs QTBUG-79126 ("Dialogs behavior on Wayland is wrong") and QTBUG-90005 ("global modality on Wayland") document that Wayland's xdg-shell protocol has no equivalent of Qt::ApplicationModal. Calling setModal(true) (which 0.7.49 added) is a no-op on Wayland except for an aggravating side effect: KWin/Wayland routes click events into the modal-grab handler which then drops them. The role-based dispatch path inside QDialogButtonBox is a known second aggravator on the same bug. The Background Tasks dialog has been working all along on the same parent-window flags — by being non-modal, with a plain QPushButton whose clicked() is wired directly to QDialog::close. Both About dialogs and the Roadmap dialog now follow that proven shape: non-modal, plain QPushButton, direct clicked() → close(). No more setModal(true), no more QDialogButtonBox. The OK / Close click reaches its slot and the dialog dismisses on the first try.

description.p: Three follow-up fixes to user reports against 0.7.48 — the About menu's OK buttons silently no-op'd again, the GitHub Public/Private badge wanted to live next to the git branch on the left, and the Background Tasks chip showed phantom counts of tasks that had long since exited.
Fixed:

ul.li: Both Help → About dialogs now dismiss on the OK button. The 0.7.35 attempt at this bug only swapped the dialog type from QMessageBox to a custom QDialog; both dialogs still went through exec(). On KDE/KWin + Qt 6.11 with the frameless + WA_TranslucentBackground MainWindow, the nested QEventLoop opened by exec() does not surface the dialog as the active input window — the OK button never receives focus and the click silently no-ops. Both handlers now use the heap + WA_DeleteOnClose + show() + raise() + activateWindow() pattern that the Background Tasks, Settings, and Claude Transcript dialogs already use successfully under the same parent-window flags. The OK button's clicked() signal is also wired directly to QDialog::accept alongside the existing QDialogButtonBox::accepted connection. The "About Qt" entry dropped QMessageBox::aboutQt for a custom QDialog with the same heap+show treatment, surfacing the Qt runtime + build versions and a link to qt.io/licensing.

Background Tasks chip now reflects only tasks that are genuinely running. The transcript-only completion detection used through 0.7.48 only marked tasks finished when Claude Code recorded a matching KillShell event or a BashOutput tool_result with status completed/killed/failed — tasks that were spawned and never polled stayed running indefinitely (the user-reported "12 tasks" phantom count). parseTranscript now performs a liveness sweep at the end: each unfinished task whose output file no longer exists OR whose mtime is older than a 60 s staleness window is flipped to finished. ClaudeBgTaskTracker::rescan was promoted to public slots: and the 2 s status timer drives refreshBgTasksButton so the sweep keeps firing while the transcript is silent. The chip falls to 0 within ~60 s of the last task going idle.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: GitHub Public/Private badge moved to the left side of the status bar, next to the git-branch chip. Was on the right (addPermanentWidget, shipped 0.7.45) where it sat alongside the Claude Code chrome; the user asked 2026-04-27 to move it next to the branch where the two badges read as a "branch · visibility" pair. Restyled to match the branch chip — same rounded bgSecondary fill + border-1px frame — while keeping the green-for-public / red-for-private foreground colour from 0.7.45.

description.p: Two tab-bar fixes the user spotted after 0.7.47 shipped. The per-tab Claude Code activity dot was only painting on one tab — the most recently focused one — even when several tabs each had their own running Claude Code session. The dot also sat too close to the tab text. The first is a real correctness bug; the second is comfort.
Fixed:

ul.li: Per-tab Claude state dot now renders on every tab with a live Claude Code session, not just the most recently focused one. ClaudeTabTracker::detectClaudeChild was assigning each shell the system-wide newest *.jsonl as its transcript path on first detection, so all N tabs running Claude ended up watching the same file and the inverse path-to-pid map was last-write-wins. The 0.7.44 walk-up logic from ClaudeIntegration::activeSessionPath is now extracted as a static helper ClaudeIntegration::sessionPathForCwd(cwd) and called from the tracker, with cwd taken from /proc/<claudePid>/cwd (falling back to /proc/<shellPid>/cwd). Two shells in two distinct project trees now end up with two distinct transcript paths and two independent state streams.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: Tab leading-edge gutter widened from 16 px to 22 px (QTabBar::tab stylesheet → padding: 6px 16px 6px 22px). The Claude state dot's center moves from r.left() + 8 to r.left() + 11 so it stays gutter-centered, leaving roughly 7 px between the dot's right edge and the first character of the tab text instead of the previous 4 px. Tabs without a Claude indicator pick up the same widened leading padding; the bar still looks balanced.

description.p: Pinned by INV-8 in tests/features/claude_tab_status_indicator/spec.md: a source-grep half asserts the tracker calls sessionPathForCwd and reads /proc/<pid>/cwd; a round-trip half builds a synthetic ~/.claude/projects/ tree with two encoded subdirs and verifies each cwd resolves to its own subdir's .jsonl.

Smaller-footprint update checks plus a pre-update warning dialog. Two pieces of user feedback after 0.7.46 shipped: the hourly background check was too noisy, and clicking the update badge to kick AppImageUpdate should warn that the terminal will need to be quit and re-launched (active Claude Code sessions will be disconnected). Both addressed in this release.
Added:

ul.li: A new Help → Check for Updates menu action with the objectName helpCheckForUpdatesAction. The result lands as a status-bar message ("Up to date" / "Update check failed: ..." / the existing badge-show flow when a newer release exists).

A pre-update confirmation QMessageBox in handleUpdateClicked. The dialog explains that AppImageUpdate writes the new release alongside the running binary, that the user will need to quit and re-launch to use it, and that active Claude Code sessions will be disconnected and need to be reconnected. Cancel short-circuits the spawn and surfaces "Update cancelled." in the status bar.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: checkForUpdates(bool userInitiated = false). The new userInitiated parameter scopes whether negative results are surfaced — startup probes stay silent so launch noise stays low; manual clicks from the Help menu surface their result so the user sees something happen.

description.p: Removed:

ul.li: The hourly m_updateCheckTimer background poll. Updates are now checked at startup (5 s after launch) and on demand via the Help menu only.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/github_status_bar/ — extended from 16 to 17 invariants. INV-9 (revised) enforces the absence of the hourly timer plus the presence of the menu action plus the userInitiated flag plumbing. INV-17 (new) walks handleUpdateClicked asserting the dialog construction precedes the startDetached call, the dialog text mentions "quit and re-launch" + "Claude Code" + "reconnected", and Cancel surfaces a status message.

Phase B of the auto-update story — actual in-place binary updates via AppImageUpdate and zsync. The 0.7.45 status bar notifier knew about new releases but only opened a download page; this release closes the loop. Click the update badge and the AppImage updates itself. v0.7.46 is the first release whose binary can be updated in place; pre-0.7.46 binaries shipped without the embedded metadata and continue to be manual-download only.
Added:

ul.li: Update-information ELF note plus a .zsync sidecar in every release. The build workflow now passes a UPDATE_INFORMATION environment variable to linuxdeploy that uses the GitHub-releases-zsync schema; linuxdeploy embeds the string into the AppImage's update-info ELF note and produces the sidecar alongside. Both files are uploaded to each release.

A new click handler on the update-available label, MainWindow::handleUpdateClicked, wired via linkActivated. Probes for AppImageUpdate (GUI) first, appimageupdatetool (CLI) second; reads the $APPIMAGE env var to find the on-disk AppImage path; runs the updater detached via QProcess::startDetached. Falls back to opening the release page in the user's browser only when neither tool is installed or the binary isn't running as an AppImage.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: The update-available label now uses setOpenExternalLinks(false); clicks route through the new handler instead of being auto-opened by Qt.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/github_status_bar/ — extended from 12 to 16 invariants. INV-11 (revised) requires the linkActivated connection. INV-13 source-greps the workflow for the UPDATE_INFORMATION env var with the right schema. INV-14 confirms the .zsync sidecar upload. INV-15 walks the new handler asserting both updater names, the $APPIMAGE read, and the browser fallback. INV-16 confirms QProcess::startDetached is used so the updater outlives the parent process.

Notes: AppImage updates write a fresh file alongside the original (with a .zs-old suffix on the previous version) — the running process keeps the old contents in memory until the user quits and relaunches. The first release that can use this feature is 0.7.46 itself; earlier AppImages were built without the metadata.

GitHub-aware status bar — two new badges that surface context the user previously had to alt-tab to a browser to see. A Public/Private repo visibility badge for the active tab's project, and an "↗ Update vX.Y.Z available" notifier that checks the GitHub releases API hourly and lets the user click through to the release page when a newer version exists.
Added:

ul.li: Public/Private repo badge — a small QLabel next to the Roadmap button. Theme-derived colour, green for public, amber for private. Per-tab via refreshStatusBarForActiveTab calling a new refreshRepoVisibility(): walks the active tab's shellCwd() up for a .git ancestor, parses [remote "origin"] from .git/config (handles both the HTTPS and SSH URL forms GitHub uses), and resolves visibility via the gh repo view CLI with the minimal --json visibility field set. 10-minute cache by repo root. Failure modes (no .git, non-GitHub origin, gh missing or unauthenticated, network error) all hide the label.

Clickable update notifier — a cyan QLabel with setOpenExternalLinks(true) shown when a newer GitHub release exists. A new checkForUpdates() slot queries the project's releases/latest endpoint via QNetworkAccessManager, parses tag_name, and compares against ANTS_VERSION via the new pure helper compareSemver. Hourly timer plus a 5 s singleShot on startup. Click opens the release page in the user's browser.
Pure helpers added to mainwindow.cpp: findGitRepoRoot, parseGithubOriginSlug, and compareSemver.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/github_status_bar/ — 12 invariants covering label declaration, construction, object-name, hidden-default state, helper presence, both URL-form handling, refreshStatusBarForActiveTab wiring, four hide-on-failure branches in refreshRepoVisibility, the 10-minute cache TTL, the minimal gh invocation, the 60-min timer plus 5 s singleShot, the releases-latest endpoint plus User-Agent header, setOpenExternalLinks(true), and component-wise integer comparison in compareSemver.

Out of scope: actual binary auto-update via AppImageUpdate or zsync. That's a follow-up release that needs the build workflow to publish a .zsync sidecar and embed the GitHub-releases zsync update-information string in the linuxdeploy command. This release only notifies; the user clicks through to download.

Two themed cleanups in one bundle. Retiring the dormant GPU-accelerated glyph-atlas renderer (compiled-but-unreachable since 0.7.4) and its no-op user-facing chrome (Settings checkbox + View menu action) — ~900 LoC removed, retires ROADMAP § "Renderer subsystem decision". Plus scoping the Background Tasks dialog to the active tab's project tree so sessions running in another project's window don't leak into this window's button.
Changed:

ul.li: Background Tasks button now scopes to the active tab's project. Previously ClaudeIntegration::activeSessionPath() walked ~/.claude/projects/ system-wide and returned the most-recently-modified .jsonl, which meant a busy session in one project's window would surface its bg-tasks count on a sibling window pointed at a different project. The method now accepts a projectCwd argument; the active tab's shellCwd() is encoded via encodeProjectPath and the helper walks up the cwd probing each ancestor's ~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/ subdir, returning the deepest match's newest .jsonl.

description.p: Removed:

ul.li: src/glrenderer.{h,cpp} (~900 LoC). The optional GPU-accelerated glyph-atlas renderer with GLSL 3.3 shaders + instanced quads + a per-cell vertex buffer. Disabled in 0.7.4 when TerminalWidget switched from QOpenGLWidget to plain QWidget; sat compiled-but-unreachable for 39 releases.

gpu_rendering Config key + Settings checkbox + View menu action. All three wrote to the dormant bool with no observable effect since 0.7.4. Removed in this release. Existing config files with "gpu_rendering": true are silently ignored — Config doesn't validate unknown JSON keys.
TerminalWidget::setGpuRendering(bool) / gpuRendering() + the m_glRenderer / m_gpuRendering members.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/claude_bg_tasks_button/ — extended from 10 to 11 invariants. INV-11 is a three-part check on the project-scoping fix: header signature accepts projectCwd, implementation walks up via cdUp + encodeProjectPath, and MainWindow::refreshBgTasksButton reads focusedTerminal()->shellCwd() and passes it through to activeSessionPath(cwd). tests/features/settings_restore_defaults/spec.md updated to drop the no-longer-applicable gpuRendering off clause.

Roadmap viewer polish — the dialog the previous release introduced gets a working Close button and a dynamic section-navigation sidebar that lists every heading in the active ROADMAP.md and lets the user click to jump. The TOC is built per project from whatever headings the file happens to have (no hardcoded section names) so the same dialog adapts to every repo that opens with a roadmap.
Added:

ul.li: TOC sidebar in the Roadmap viewer. QSplitter(Qt::Horizontal) inside RoadmapDialog puts a QListWidget (objectName "roadmap-toc") on the left and the rendered viewer on the right. The list is rebuilt from the markdown on every refresh — including live-update events on ROADMAP.md change — and entries are indented two spaces per level above 1 with level-1 headings shown bold. Click or activate an entry → the viewer scrolls to the matching heading.

Anchor-emitting renderer. RoadmapDialog::renderHtml now prepends before each

/

/

/

it emits. The N-th anchor matches the N-th entry returned by the new pure helper RoadmapDialog::extractToc, which returns a QVector of {level, text, anchor} triples. Both helpers are pure and static so they're driven by the feature-conformance test without spinning a Qt widget tree.

QTextBrowser in place of QTextEdit. Required for scrollToAnchor support. Links are pinned non-navigable (setOpenLinks(false) / setOpenExternalLinks(false)) so a stray markdown link can't replace the document.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Close button in the Roadmap viewer is now actually clickable. The dialog used the role-based QDialogButtonBox::rejected signal alone; under some Qt 6 builds the standard Close button does not trigger that signal, leaving the button looking active but doing nothing. The Close button is now wired both via rejected() (kept as belt-and-braces) and via a direct clicked connection on the underlying QPushButton retrieved with button(QDialogButtonBox::Close). The direct connect is the authoritative fix.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/roadmap_viewer/ — extended from 10 to 14 invariants. INV-11 verifies extractToc returns a 5-entry list with matching levels, raw text, and roadmap-toc-N anchors against a known multi-level input. INV-12 verifies renderHtml emits the anchor before each heading. INV-13 source-greps for QSplitter + roadmap-toc objectName + scrollToAnchor in the dialog. INV-14 source-greps the Close-button direct QAbstractButton::clicked connection.

Inline ghost-text completion in the Command Palette — Claude Code's /slash-command UX, ported to Ctrl+Shift+P. As you type, the unmatched suffix of the top fuzzy-match is rendered in a dimmed colour right after the cursor; Tab commits the suggestion into the input. Retires ROADMAP § "Command Palette ghost-completion (near-term, small scope)".
Added:

ul.li: GhostLineEdit subclass of QLineEdit. Lives in src/commandpalette.h. Carries a m_ghost string set via setGhostSuffix(); paintEvent calls QLineEdit::paintEvent(event) first, then opens a fresh QPainter and draws the ghost at cursorRect().right() + 1 using palette().color(QPalette::Text) with setAlphaF(0.45). No layout changes — the suffix overlays the existing line-edit content area, so palette geometry is unchanged.

CommandPalette::updateGhostCompletion(filter). Invoked from populateList after the result list is rebuilt. Looks at m_list->item(0), recovers the underlying QAction, strips & accelerators, and: if the filter is empty, list is empty, or the action name does not start with the filter (case-insensitive), setGhostSuffix(""); otherwise setGhostSuffix(name.mid(filter.length())) (preserving the action name's original casing in the ghost). contains()-only matches get an empty ghost — the visual contract is that the suffix appears flush after the user's typed input, which only makes sense for prefix matches.

Tab-key commit handler. eventFilter adds Qt::Key_Tab → commitGhost(), which appends the ghost suffix to the input via setText; the follow-up textChanged → filterActions → populateList → updateGhostCompletion cycle clears the ghost in the same dispatch since the new filter equals the action name's tail exactly. The post-commit text equals the visible composition (user-typed prefix + ghost suffix), so user-typed casing is preserved — same as shell-completion semantics. Tab is always consumed by the palette (even when the ghost is empty) so focus cannot leak out of the input. Tab does not also execute the action — the user presses Enter to run, matching Claude Code's /slash-completion contract.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/command_palette_ghost_completion/ — ten invariants on the GhostLineEdit existence, ghost-empty vs ghost-set transitions across empty / prefix / uppercase-prefix / contains-only / no-match filters, Tab commit semantics (text and ghost state), Tab-with-empty-ghost focus retention, source-grep on the dimmed-color literal and cursorRect anchor, and Esc dismiss regression. Drives CommandPalette directly under QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen. Pre-fix verification: with the implementation stashed, the test fails to even compile (missing GhostLineEdit symbol). Out of scope: the in-terminal shell ghost-suggestion (fish-style, OSC 133 prompt-detection + history scraping) remains 💭 in ROADMAP, deferred past 1.0.

Accessibility. Every glyph-only chrome control now carries an explicit setAccessibleName and setAccessibleDescription, so Orca, Speakup, and other AT-SPI screen readers announce them by purpose ("Close window") instead of by codepoint name ("eight-spoked asterisk", "multiplication sign x"). Two ROADMAP items retired in one bundle: § "Accessibility pass on chrome" (mainwindow / palette / title-bar control labels) and § "AT-SPI introspection lane" (an automated check that every user-visible chrome control carries a name).
Added:

ul.li: Accessible names on TitleBar window controls. centerBtn ("Center window"), minimizeBtn ("Minimize window"), maximizeBtn ("Maximize window" / "Maximize or restore this window"), and closeBtn ("Close window") each get an explicit setAccessibleName + setAccessibleDescription set immediately after setText in titlebar.cpp:54-97. Each button also gains a stable objectName so the introspection test can find them deterministically. Visual rendering is unchanged — the glyphs (✥ – □ ✕) still drive the on-screen pixels; only the AT-SPI tree changed.

Accessible names on the Command Palette. The search input (objectName commandPaletteInput) gets Command palette search / Type to filter actions; Tab to commit; Esc to dismiss; the result list (objectName commandPaletteList) gets Command palette results / Available actions matching the current filter. Placeholder text and visible behaviour are unchanged: the placeholder is a typing hint for sighted users; the accessible name is what screen readers announce on focus.

description.p: Locked by a new tests/features/a11y_chrome_names/ spec + test (eight invariants — T1–T4 each TitleBar button has the right accessibleName + non-empty accessibleDescription; T5/T6 the CommandPalette input + list have theirs; T7 the AT-SPI introspection lane sweeps every reachable QAbstractButton/QLineEdit in the chrome tree; T8/T9 source-grep counts on titlebar.cpp/commandpalette.cpp so a refactor that drops the calls fails the build, not just AT-SPI usage at runtime). The test runs under QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen, so it works in CI without a display. tr() translation hooks are deferred to the 0.9.0 H10 i18n bundle.

Performance. Two Tier 3 perf items from ROADMAP.md § 0.7.x ⚡ / 🏗 Tier 3 — structural retired in one bundle: scroll-region rotate plus a zero-copy VtBatch hop across the worker→GUI thread boundary. Both are observably equivalent at the API level — the existing tests/features/scroll_region_rotate/ and tests/features/threaded_parse_equivalence/ regressions pass unchanged — so the win is throughput / cost, not visible behaviour.
Changed:

ul.li: TerminalGrid::scrollUp / scrollDown use std::rotate. Replaced the per-iteration m_screenLines.erase + insert loop with a single rotate over [scrollTop, scrollBottom], gated by a pool-salvage / scrollback-push pre-pass and a fresh-blank post-pass. The CSI 2J doubling-guard window check now runs once per batch (microsecond-scale elapsed time made the per-iteration window-extend redundant). m_scrollback cap enforcement collapses from N pop_front passes to one. On CSI 100 S against an 80-row screen, the row-shift cost goes from O(count × rows) memmoves (8000) to O(rows) (80).

VtStream::batchReady carries VtBatchPtr instead of const VtBatch &. New alias using VtBatchPtr = std::shared_ptr<const VtBatch>; in vtstream.h. Qt's queued-connection plumbing must own dispatched parameters, so the prior const T & signal forced a deep copy of the entire batch (the actions vector + rawBytes QByteArray) on every worker→GUI hop. The shared_ptr wrap reduces the cross-thread payload to a 16-byte atomic refcount bump; the underlying VtBatch lives on the heap and is not duplicated. Both emit sites (flushBatch, onPtyFinished) build via std::make_shared<VtBatch>(); TerminalWidget::onVtBatch signature is now void onVtBatch(VtBatchPtr batch) with field access via batch->….

description.p: Locked by a new tests/features/vtbatch_zero_copy/ spec + test (5 source-grep invariants pinning the cross-thread signal shape so a future refactor cannot silently revert to the deep-copy form). Behavioural equivalence on the action stream stays covered by tests/features/threaded_parse_equivalence/; rotation correctness stays covered by the 8 algorithm-agnostic invariants in tests/features/scroll_region_rotate/.

Claude Code UX bundle plus a status-bar Roadmap viewer. Two user requests landed in quick succession. Request 1: "Let's have a round dot on each tab that has a Claude Code session running (no icons or anything else other than the tab label). The dot will change colour with the various states that Claude Code is in. Each state has its own colour (grey for idle). Then extend those colours to the status bar Claude Code status too." Request 2: "Add a button on the status bar to view the roadmap. So, it brings up a dialog showing the roadmap. It should have filters as well to show what is outstanding and what is completed. If at all possible, it should also highlight what item is being done currently. The roadmap button should only show if there is roadmap documentation — let's simplify that to requiring a roadmap.md file only."
Added:

ul.li: Unified Claude state-dot palette across tabs and status bar. New static helper ClaudeTabIndicator::color(Glyph) in coloredtabbar.h is the single source of truth for an eight-state palette: Idle grey, Thinking blue, ToolUse yellow, Bash green, Planning cyan, Auditing magenta, Compacting violet, AwaitingInput orange. Red is intentionally absent — AwaitingInput is a normal interaction state, not an error. ColoredTabBar::paintEvent uses a single kDotRadius = 4 for every state; the prior AwaitingInput "outline + radius 5" treatment is removed. The bottom status-bar Claude Code label was rewired to map current state through the same helper, replacing prior Theme::ansi[] mappings that drifted from the tab dot's colour.

Auditing now lights the per-tab dot. Plumbed into ClaudeTabTracker::ShellState::auditing (mirrored from ClaudeTranscriptSnapshot.auditing); tab provider routes it to Glyph::Auditing magenta. Precedence chain unchanged across both surfaces: AwaitingInput → Planning → Auditing → state-derived.

Status-bar Roadmap viewer button (roadmapdialog.{h,cpp}). Visible iff the active tab's shellCwd() contains a ROADMAP.md (case-insensitive). Click opens a non-modal RoadmapDialog with five peer category checkboxes (Done, Planned, In progress, Considered, Currently being tackled), all default-checked. The static RoadmapDialog::renderHtml helper is pure so tests can drive it without a Qt widget.

"Currently being tackled" highlight. A bullet matches the current-work signal if its first-80-character normalised payload contains a phrase from a signal set built from the local CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] bullets and the last 5 non-release/non-merge/non-revert git commit subjects. Matched bullets get a yellow left-border CSS highlight (the same ToolUse yellow from the dot palette). When the signal set is empty, no highlight renders.

Live-tail dialog mechanics. QFileSystemWatcher on ROADMAP.md and CHANGELOG.md, 200 ms debounce, plus the same scroll-preservation triple shipped with 0.7.37 / 0.7.38: m_lastHtml shared_ptr cache + skip-identical-HTML guard, capture vbar before setHtml, restore with qMin(saved, vbar->maximum()) clamp, was-at-bottom pin so live appends stay visible.

description.p: Changed: ClaudeTabIndicator::Glyph enum gains Auditing between Planning and Compacting; ClaudeTabTracker::ShellState gains a bool auditing field with a comparison branch in maybeEmit. MainWindow::applyClaudeStatusLabel no longer reads Theme::ansi[] for Claude-label colours — all state → colour mappings now route through the unified helper. Removed: AwaitingInput-specific dot decoration (radius 5 + white outline); colour alone is the differentiator now.
Locked by tests/features/claude_state_dot_palette/ (8 invariants) and tests/features/roadmap_viewer/ (10 hybrid invariants — source-grep plus behavioural drives of the static renderHtml helper).

Feature. Background-tasks status-bar surface for Claude Code. User request 2026-04-25: "a button on the status bar when there are background tasks being run. We then click the button to view what Claude Code shows for the background tasks. The button opens a dialog showing the live update info on the background tasks." Claude Code can spawn long-running tasks via Bash or Task with run_in_background: true. The TUI shows a sidebar listing them with their tail output; users dropping into a different tab to keep working had no way to see those tasks from Ants Terminal — they had to flip back to the TUI and parse the sidebar by hand.
Added:

ul.li: Background-tasks tracker. A pure parser walks the active session's transcript JSONL, recognizing tool_use blocks whose input.run_in_background == true (start) and tool_result blocks whose toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId is set (confirmation, with the on-disk output path extracted from the result body). Completion / kill state is derived from subsequent BashOutput results carrying status: "completed" | "killed" | "failed" and from KillShell tool calls. Static parseTranscript(path) is testable without a watcher.

Status-bar button. Sibling to the Review Changes button; same fixed sizePolicy contract. Hidden by default; visible only while the running count is greater than zero for the active tab's session. Label re-renders as "Background Tasks (N)"; tooltip discloses running + total. Re-targeted on tab switch via the unified refreshStatusBarForActiveTab path, so each tab's session drives its own count independently.

Live-tail dialog. Mirrors the Review Changes live-update model that 0.7.37 stabilized. QFileSystemWatcher covers each task's .output file plus the transcript path; a 200 ms debounce timer collapses bursts of fileChanged signals into one render. Skip-identical-HTML guard plus capture-vbar-before-setHtml + restore-after with qMin(..., maximum()) clamp keeps absolute scroll position when content changes. Bonus "was at bottom" detection: when the user is tailing the bottom, pin them to the bottom across appends so live output stays visible without manual scrolling.

description.p: Locked by a new tests/features/claude_bg_tasks_button/ spec + test (10 invariants — source-grep harness, no Qt link).

UX bug fix. Live-update regression in the Review Changes dialog (the QFileSystemWatcher + 300ms debounce wiring added in 0.7.32). User report 2026-04-25: "When using the Review Changes dialog, the constant resetting of the text means that if I scroll, it resets to the beginning every refresh. That means I can't scroll basically." The 0.7.32 finalize lambda called setHtml unconditionally on every probe completion, snapping the QTextEdit scroll bar back to the top — on a long diff with active live updates the dialog became unscrollable.
Fixed:

ul.li: Review Changes dialog: scroll position preserved across live refreshes. Two-layer guard around the setHtml call inside MainWindow::showDiffViewer's finalize lambda. (1) An auto lastHtml = std::make_shared<QString>() cache threaded through runProbes and finalize lets finalize early-return when the new render is byte-identical to the previous one — the common case during idle live-update tics. Selection, cursor, and scroll are byte-perfectly preserved. (2) When content does change, vertical and horizontal scroll-bar values are captured before setHtml and restored after, clamped to bar->maximum() so a shorter render after a commit doesn't over-scroll. First-render carve-out (isFirstRender = lastHtml->isEmpty()) keeps the initial paint at the top.

description.p: Locked by a new tests/features/review_changes_scroll_preserve/ spec + test (6 invariants — source-grep harness scoped to the showDiffViewer body).

UX bug fix. Same translucent-parent failure mode that bit the menubar in 0.7.25/0.7.26 caught in two more places (user report, post-0.7.32): "The tabs themselves are fine but the rest of the tab bar across the window is transparent as well as the background for the status bar." The 0.7.32 close-button SVG drew the user's eye to the empty-area fill that had been mis-painted all along; the bug was not introduced by 0.7.32 but became visible because the new tab look re-balanced what the user noticed.
Fixed:

ul.li: Tab bar empty area paints opaque under WA_TranslucentBackground. Was: the strip to the right of the last tab rendered the desktop wallpaper through, with the QSS QTabBar { background-color: ... } rule silently dropped by Qt's stylesheet engine on KWin + Breeze + Qt 6 once WA_OpaquePaintEvent was set on the widget — same root cause documented in opaquemenubar.h. Now: ColoredTabBar exposes setBackgroundFill(QColor) and prepends a CompositionMode_Source fillRect to its existing paintEvent, before the base class draws tabs and before the colour-group gradient overlay. applyTheme() pushes theme.bgSecondary into the override.

Status bar background paints opaque under WA_TranslucentBackground. Was: the entire status bar strip rendered the desktop wallpaper through — same root cause as the tab bar above. Now: a header-only OpaqueStatusBar mirrors OpaqueMenuBar (paintEvent fillRect → delegate). MainWindow constructs an OpaqueStatusBar, installs it via setStatusBar(...) before the first statusBar() call (Qt's lazy-creation would otherwise install a plain QStatusBar that paints transparent), and pushes theme.bgSecondary via setBackgroundFill from applyTheme().

description.p: Locked by a new tests/features/tabbar_statusbar_opaque/ spec + test (5 invariants — source-grep harness, no display required).
UX bug fix. Single user-reported regression in the Help → About Ants Terminal dialog (the GUI version indicator added in 0.7.22).
Fixed:

ul.li: About Ants Terminal dialog OK button silently no-op'd. Was: Help → About Ants Terminal… opened correctly, but clicking OK did nothing — the dialog could only be dismissed via the window-manager close button. The 0.7.22 implementation used QMessageBox::Ok with Qt::TextBrowserInteraction (which pulls in Qt::TextSelectableByMouse); under the combination of our frameless MainWindow, Qt::WA_TranslucentBackground, KDE Plasma + KWin, and Qt 6.11 this caused the OK click to be silently dropped. Now: the About handler builds a custom QDialog with a QDialogButtonBox(QDialogButtonBox::Ok) whose accepted signal is explicitly connected to QDialog::accept — standard, testable, independent of QMessageBox's internal standard-button dispatch.

About dialog GitHub link was a visual-only no-op. Was: the link was rendered as clickable (cursor changed on hover) because Qt::TextBrowserInteraction enables Qt::LinksAccessibleByMouse, but setOpenExternalLinks(true) was never called on the label — clicking emitted linkActivated() into the void. Now: the body QLabel has setOpenExternalLinks(true), so clicking the GitHub URL opens it in the user's browser.

description.p: Locked by an updated tests/features/help_about_menu/ spec + test (8 invariants — pre-fix source fails 6 of them).

Terminal correctness. One ROADMAP item retiring the last known DECOM (origin mode) bug — tmux/screen save-restore round-trips were silently dropping the origin-mode flag and ignoring scroll-region-relative coordinates on absolute cursor moves.
Fixed:

ul.li: CUP / HVP / VPA translate to scroll-region origin under DECOM. Was: with origin mode (CSI ?6h) active and a scroll region set via DECSTBM, CSI 1;1H jumped to absolute (0, 0) and CSI 99;1H clamped to the bottom of the entire screen instead of the bottom of the scroll region. Programs that depend on origin-mode-relative positioning (vim split-window cursor restoration, screen's caption line, tmux status redraws) wrote to the wrong row. Now: the 'H', 'f', and 'd' cases add m_scrollTop to the requested row when m_originMode is set, then clamp to [m_scrollTop, m_scrollBottom]. Behaviour matches xterm.

DECSC saves DECOM + DECAWM, DECRC restores them. Was: saveCursor() stored only (row, col, attrs), so a TUI that flipped origin mode or auto-wrap, did CSI s/ESC 7, ran code that re-toggled the flag, then CSI u/ESC 8'd, ended up with the flag in whatever state the inner code left it — silent coordinate-space corruption. Now: new m_savedOriginMode and m_savedAutoWrap members capture the flags on save and restore them on restore, matching the VT420 spec.

DECSTBM home position respects DECOM. Was: setScrollRegion() always homed the cursor to absolute (0, 0). Now: when m_originMode is on at the time DECSTBM lands, the cursor moves to (m_scrollTop, 0) — the top-left of the origin-mode coordinate space — as xterm does.

description.p: Locked by tests/features/origin_mode_correctness/ — 12 behavioural invariants plus 4 source-grep checks.
Lifecycle / cleanup. Three ROADMAP items addressing GUI-thread blocking on shutdown, an xdg-desktop-portal session leak, and two latent issues (OOM surface + symlink escape) in the Lua plugin loader.
Changed:

ul.li: PTY destructor escalation runs off the main thread. Was: SIGTERM-then-busy-wait-then-SIGKILL ran on the GUI thread; N split panes closing together blocked window close N × 500 ms. Now: the SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation moves to a detached std::thread capturing the pid by value. Cheap pre-escalation reap (SIGHUP + close master fd + WNOHANG) stays inline so well-behaved children don't pay a thread spawn. Synchronous fallback retained for the rare case of thread-creation failure under ulimit -u pressure. Locked by tests/features/pty_dtor_off_main_thread/ with 11 invariants.

GlobalShortcutsPortal closes its session on destruction. Was: no destructor at all — the session handle leaked for the lifetime of the D-Bus client. xdg-desktop-portal accumulated one orphan session per Ants invocation that crashed or was SIGKILLed before implicit cleanup. Now: destructor issues an asynchronous org.freedesktop.portal.Session.Close call against the session handle when non-empty. Locked by tests/features/portal_session_close/ with 8 invariants.

Plugin manifest cap + canonical plugin path. Was: f.readAll() on manifest.json was unbounded (multi-GB manifest would OOM before JSON parsing); plugin scan used QDir::Dirs without NoSymLinks or canonical anchoring (a tarball symlink could redirect init.lua loading to /etc/cron.daily). Now: 1 MiB manifest cap via bounded f.read; scan anchors on QFileInfo::canonicalFilePath, passes QDir::NoSymLinks, and per-entry verifies canonical containment inside the plugin root. Locked by tests/features/plugin_manifest_safety/ with 12 invariants.

description.p: Dialog UX. Three Settings dialog ROADMAP items (dependency-UI gating, Cancel rollback for Profiles, Restore Defaults per-tab) plus a user feedback fix for the Review Changes dialog (2026-04-25): pre-fix it only surfaced current-branch state and missed work living on other branches.
Added:

ul.li: Restore Defaults per primary Settings tab. General, Appearance, Terminal, AI tabs now each have a dedicated "Restore Defaults" button that resets only that tab's controls to the schema defaults (matching Config::xxx() getter defaults). Reset slots mutate widget state only — applySettings still commits, Cancel still rolls back. Locked by tests/features/settings_restore_defaults/ with 22 invariants.

Review Changes dialog: per-branch summary + cross-branch unpushed. Was: three async git probes (status, diff HEAD, log @{u}..HEAD) all scoped to the current branch — a user with feature branches sitting on a clean branch saw "no unpushed". Now: two additional probes (git for-each-ref refs/heads + git log --branches --not --remotes) surface every local branch with its ahead/behind/gone state and every commit reachable from any local branch but not from any remote-tracking branch. Copy Diff includes both new sections.

Review Changes dialog: live updates. User feedback 2026-04-25: dialog should refresh as the user works. Was: probes ran once on dialog open and never again. Now: QFileSystemWatcher watches the working directory plus .git/HEAD, .git/index, .git/refs/heads, .git/refs/remotes, .git/logs/HEAD. A 300 ms single-shot QTimer debounces the burst of fileChanged events that git pull fires. A manual Refresh button bypasses the debounce. A live-status label confirms the watcher is wired. Probe spawning is now a re-callable runProbes lambda that constructs a fresh ProbeState per call so concurrent in-flight probes from a previous render can't poison the new one. Locked by tests/features/review_changes_branches/ — 33 invariants total covering both branch-awareness and live-update wiring.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: Tab close button (×) always visible, not hover-only. User feedback 2026-04-25. The 0.6.27 platform-style fallback still rendered hover-only on Fusion / qt6ct / some Plasma colour schemes. Now: explicit data-URI SVG image: url(...) rules in both default and :hover QTabBar::close-button variants. Glyph re-tints with the active theme; hover keeps the ansi-red will-click background. Locked by tests/features/tab_close_button_visible/ with 11 invariants.

Dependency-UI enable gating in Settings. Was: master checkboxes m_aiEnabled, m_autoColorScheme, m_quakeMode gated logic but not UI — typing an API key into a feature-disabled AI tab produced silent no-ops. Now: QCheckBox::toggled is wired to setEnabled on every dependent sibling, with a one-shot sync call at construction so the initial state matches the loaded config. Disabled controls keep their values so toggling the master back on restores the prior selection. Locked by tests/features/settings_dependency_gating/ with 16 invariants.

Profiles tab honors Cancel/OK semantics. Was: profile Save/Delete/Load buttons mutated m_config immediately, bypassing the OK/Cancel pattern every other tab follows. Now: buttons mutate a pending-state pair (m_pendingProfiles + m_pendingActiveProfile); applySettings is the single commit point. Cancel discards staged edits cleanly. Locked by tests/features/settings_profile_cancel_rollback/ with 11 invariants including a global "exactly one m_config->setProfiles call site" check.

description.p: Persistence integrity (cross-file). Four items addressing the silent-data-loss / permission-drift / concurrent-writer surfaces that span Config, ClaudeAllowlist, SessionManager, and SettingsDialog. Splits the growing secureio.h into secureio.h (perms) + configbackup.h (rotation + cooperative write lock) before a third helper landed.
Added:

ul.li: ConfigWriteLock cooperative write lock for shared config files (src/configbackup.h). Was: two simultaneously-running Ants instances saving the same ~/.config/ants-terminal/config.json raced over <path>.tmp + rename(2) — both writers truncated the shared tmp, partial bytes interleaved on the same inode, and last-rename-wins silently dropped one process's keystrokes / settings / profile / AI key. Same shape for ~/.claude/settings.json where Allowlist + Install-hooks + git-context-installer all read-modify-write with no serialization. Now: an RAII guard wraps POSIX flock(2) on a sibling <path>.lock file with a 5-second poll deadline (100 × 50 ms), advisory so cooperating callers serialize while non-cooperating editors (vim, jq) bypass by design. Config::save, ClaudeAllowlistDialog::saveSettings, SettingsDialog::installClaudeHooks, and SettingsDialog::installClaudeGitContextHook now construct ConfigWriteLock with an acquired() guard before the write block; failure to acquire logs and returns. Locked by tests/features/concurrent_writer_lock/ — 5 runtime invariants via fork(2) so flock semantics are honestly tested across processes, plus 7 source-grep invariants on each save site.

Belt-and-suspenders post-rename setOwnerOnlyPerms at every persistence site. Was: each site set 0600 on the temp fd before write but relied on rename(2) preserving perms across the swap. ext4/xfs/btrfs honor that; FAT/exFAT (no POSIX bits at all), some SMB/NFS servers (server-side rename applies server umask), and Qt's copy+unlink fallback (cross-device rename, exotic mounts) do not. Files containing ai_api_key (config.json), Claude Code bearer tokens (settings.json), or paste-buffer scrollback content (session_*.dat) could land 0644 on those filesystems and leak to every UID on the host. Now: each site re-chmods the final inode after rename / commit() returns success — idempotent on POSIX filesystems, essential elsewhere.
